body.title: CLOSED: Igor memory librarian — redirected to T-librarian-curation-tools
body.result: redirected to T-librarian-curation-tools — curation belongs in ADC Librarian device, not Igor cognition stack; proposals queue (T-igor-proposals-queue) remains the interface
body.description:
New module `wild_igor/igor/cognition/librarian.py`. Scheduled curation pass (piggybacks on dreaming interval or separate IGOR_LIBRARIAN_INTERVAL). Responsibilities: (1) identify duplicate/highly-similar clan.memories entries (same code_ref, same semantic fingerprint); (2) prune stale SEMANTIC memories with no access in N days (configurable, default 30); (3) merge overlapping PROCEDURAL habits where safe (same code_ref, slightly different conditions — merge into single entry, record provenance); (4) mark merged/pruned as archived (never hard-delete). All curation decisions logged to a librarian_log table or JSONL.

**Affected files:** `wild_igor/igor/cognition/librarian.py` (new), `wild_igor/igor/cognition/dreaming.py` (wire librarian.run() call)
**Design rules:** no-sqlite; direct psycopg2; never hard-delete (archived flag or soft-delete column); all curation decisions logged with reason; librarian disabled when IGOR_LIBRARIAN_INTERVAL=0; no cortex.py touch
**Scope boundary:** IN — clan.memories analysis + proposals to instance.proposals, curation log, dreaming wiring. OUT add: no direct clan.memories writes (proposals queue only). OUT — no instance.watch_problems pruning (separate), no cross-instance curation, no UI.
**Test plan:** librarian identifies and archives seeded duplicate memory pair; prune skips recently-accessed memories; merged memory preserves both source IDs in metadata field; curation log records each action
